# CLAUDE.md Compliance Checklist

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Purpose:** Ensure every development task follows the mandatory CLAUDE.md workflow
**Usage:** Reference this checklist BEFORE starting ANY coding task

---

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
  ```bash
  # For main branch
  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
  # OR for master branch
  git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
  ```
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template from `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**

---

## Project-Type Specific Checklists

### For UI/Visual Changes

- [ ] **Screenshot current state** before making changes
- [ ] **Make ONE change** at a time (not multiple)
- [ ] **Test in browsers:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] **Test responsiveness:** 375px, 768px, 1024px+
- [ ] **Compare to baseline** (visual diff)
- [ ] **Check animations work** (60fps target)
- [ ] **Get user approval** before committing

### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects

- [ ] **Run tests:** `npm run test`
- [ ] **Type check:** `npm run type-check`
- [ ] **Lint check:** `npm run lint`
- [ ] **Build check:** `npm run build`
- [ ] **Verify bundle size** acceptable

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

- [ ] **Manual verification** in browser
- [ ] **Check console** for errors
- [ ] **Resize window** (mobile/desktop)
- [ ] **Run Lighthouse audit** in DevTools

### For Automation/Scripts

- [ ] **Test with DRY_RUN=true** first
- [ ] **Verify output matches** expectations
- [ ] **Check validation rules** work
- [ ] **Test edge cases**
- [ ] **Update docs** if behavior changes

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md`
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
  - Time comparison (estimated vs actual)
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed
  - `docs/technical/` for new systems/patterns
  - `docs/product/` for new features
  - Any other relevant docs
- [ ] **Verify in light mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation** (accessibility)
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes** (375px, 768px, 1024px)

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md` (hook will block if missing)
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why
  - Related issues (#123)

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

---

## Workflow Rules Quick Reference

### Core Workflow Rules

| # | Rule | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | **Think → Plan → Write** | Create task file FIRST before coding |
| 2 | **Plan has todo list** | Checklist with items to check off |
| 3 | **Check before working** | Get user approval via ExitPlanMode |
| 4 | **Mark tasks complete** | Update todos as you go |
| 5 | **High-level explanations** | Don't overexplain every detail |
| 6 | **Simple changes** | Minimal code impact |
| 7 | **Add review section** | Document what changed when done |
| 8 | **No lazy fixes** | Find root cause, no temporary patches |
| 9 | **Simplicity is key** | Impact minimal code, avoid bugs |
| 10 | **Update documentation** | Keep docs detailed, clear, clean |
| 11 | **Sync with remote** | Start of EVERY session |
| 12 | **Perfect foundation** | Build for 100% even if 1% complete |
| 13 | **Complete logical units** | Never stop mid-implementation |
| 14 | **Validate current date** | Check `<env>` section for today's date |
| 15 | **Commit & Push at end** | Git add, commit with details, push |

### Core Philosophy Principles

| # | Principle | Description |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | **Correctness & Integrity** | No data loss. Security P0. |
| 2 | **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
| 3 | **Atomic Execution** | Small, reviewable steps. Explain WHY. |
| 4 | **Maintainability** | Explicit > Clever. No magic. |
| 5 | **Optimal Location** | Files must be in the most logical place. |
| 6 | **Scalable Architecture** | Build primitives (building blocks), not one-offs. |
| 7 | **Root Cause Only** | Trace bugs to source. No band-aids. |
| 8 | **Infinite Initiative** | Fix broken windows immediately. Update stale docs. |

---

## Automation Tools

### Available Helpers

- **Git Pre-Commit Hook:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
  - Blocks commits when code changes without task file updates
  - Can bypass with `--no-verify` (emergencies only)

- **Task Template:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
  - Standard format for all task files
  - Includes review section template

- **Custom Commands:** `.claude/commands/`
  - `/start-task` - Intelligent task router
  - `/run` - Execute prompts with auto-archiving

- **Automation Guide:** `.claude/docs/automation-guide.md`
  - Detailed setup instructions
  - Troubleshooting tips
  - Customization guide

- **Skills:** `.claude/skills/`
  - Various specialized Claude Code skills
  - See `.claude/skills/README.md` for list

---

## Emergency Bypass

### When to Override

Use `git commit --no-verify` ONLY for:
- Hotfix deployments (site down)
- Reverting broken commits
- Critical security issues
- Documentation-only changes (maybe)

**NOT for:**
- "I'm in a hurry"
- "I'll update task file later"
- "It's just a small change"

**Otherwise:** Always follow the full checklist.

---

## Questions?

- See: `CLAUDE.md` for full workflow guide
- See: `.claude/docs/automation-guide.md` for automation help
- See: `.claude/skills/README.md` for available skills
- See: `tasks/` directory for example task files

---

**Remember:** These rules exist to ensure:
- Quality code
- Complete documentation
- No forgotten tasks
- Consistent workflow
- Professional results

**Following this checklist = Better code + Better outcomes**

1→# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide
     2→
     3→**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
     4→**Last Updated:** December 2025
     5→**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Overview](#overview)
    12→2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
    13→3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
    14→4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
    15→5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
    16→6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    17→7. [Customization](#customization)
    18→8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
    19→9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Overview
    24→
    25→This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:
    26→
    27→- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    28→- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    29→- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    30→- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    31→
    32→**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    33→
    34→### Supported Project Types
    35→
    36→This guide supports multiple project configurations:
    37→
    38→| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
    39→|--------------|------------|-------------|
    40→| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
    41→| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
    42→| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
    43→| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |
    44→
    45→---
    46→
    47→## Quick Start
    48→
    49→### Before Starting ANY Task
    50→
    51→1. **Sync with main branch:**
    52→   ```bash
    53→   # For main branch
    54→   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
    55→
    56→   # For master branch
    57→   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
    58→   ```
    59→
    60→2. **Check workflow checklist:**
    61→   ```bash
    62→   cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
    63→   ```
    64→
    65→3. **Create task file from template:**
    66→   ```bash
    67→   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    68→   ```
    69→
    70→4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
    71→   Use `ExitPlanMode` tool or ask user before coding
    72→
    73→### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)
    74→
    75→```bash
    76→# Start local server
    77→python3 -m http.server 8000
    78→# Or: npx serve .
    79→# Visit: http://localhost:8000
    80→```
    81→
    82→### For Node.js/React Projects
    83→
    84→```bash
    85→# Install dependencies
    86→npm install
    87→
    88→# Start development server
    89→npm run dev
    90→
    91→# Run tests
    92→npm run test
    93→
    94→# Type check (TypeScript)
    95→npm run type-check
    96→
    97→# Build
    98→npm run build
    99→```
   100→
   101→### For Automation Scripts
   102→
   103→```bash
   104→# Test with dry run first
   105→DRY_RUN=true python automation/[script].py
   106→
   107→# Verify output before running live
   108→```
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Automation Tools
   113→
   114→### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
   115→
   116→**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   117→**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
   118→**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
   119→
   120→**How it works:**
   121→1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
   122→2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
   123→3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
   124→4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
   125→
   126→**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
   127→```
   128→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
   129→  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
   130→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
   131→
   132→Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
   133→
   134→Changed code files:
   135→  - src/components/Modal.tsx
   136→  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   137→
   138→Required Action:
   139→  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
   140→  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
   141→  3. Retry commit: git commit
   142→```
   143→
   144→**Emergency Bypass:**
   145→```bash
   146→git commit --no-verify
   147→```
   148→**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→### 2. Workflow Checklist
   153→
   154→**Location:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
   155→**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
   156→**Enforces:** All workflow rules
   157→
   158→**When to use:**
   159→- Before starting ANY task
   160→- During implementation (reference as needed)
   161→- Before marking work complete
   162→- Before creating git commits
   163→
   164→**Sections:**
   165→- Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
   166→- During Implementation
   167→- When Complete
   168→- Git Commit Checklist
   169→- Quick Reference for all rules
   170→
   171→**View checklist:**
   172→```bash
   173→cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
   174→```
   175→
   176→---
   177→
   178→### 3. Task Template
   179→
   180→**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   181→**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
   182→**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7
   183→
   184→**Sections included:**
   185→- Overview and problem statement
   186→- Files to read
   187→- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
   188→- Todo checklist with time estimates
   189→- Design considerations
   190→- Implementation notes
   191→- Testing checklist
   192→- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)
   193→
   194→**Create new task:**
   195→```bash
   196→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   197→```
   198→
   199→**Good naming examples:**
   200→- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
   201→- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
   202→- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`
   203→- `tasks/optimize-image-loading.md`
   204→
   205→**Bad naming examples:**
   206→- `tasks/todo.md`
   207→- `tasks/task1.md`
   208→- `tasks/new.md`
   209→- `tasks/stuff.md`
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→### 4. Custom Commands
   214→
   215→**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
   216→**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow
   217→
   218→#### `/start-task` Command
   219→
   220→**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
   221→**Purpose:** Intelligent task router
   222→
   223→**What it covers:**
   224→1. Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
   225→2. Creates task files from templates
   226→3. Checks existing todos
   227→4. Initiates planning mode
   228→5. Gets user approval before implementation
   229→
   230→**Use when:**
   231→- Starting a new task
   232→- Unsure of the workflow
   233→- Need a quick reference
   234→
   235→#### `/run` Command
   236→
   237→**File:** `.claude/commands/run.md`
   238→**Purpose:** Execute prompts with auto-archiving
   239→
   240→---
   241→
   242→## Setup Instructions
   243→
   244→### File Structure (Generic)
   245→
   246→```
   247→[project-root]/
   248→├── .git/
   249→│   └── hooks/
   250→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   251→├── .claude/
   252→│   ├── CLAUDE.md                   (global instructions)
   253→│   ├── README.md                   (folder overview)
   254→│   ├── settings.json               (Claude settings)
   255→│   ├── settings.local.json         (local overrides)
   256→│   ├── docs/
   257→│   │   ├── automation-guide.md     (this file)
   258→│   │   └── workflow-checklist.md   (step-by-step checklist)
   259→│   ├── templates/
   260→│   │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
   261→│   ├── commands/
   262→│   │   └── start-task.md           (custom commands)
   263→│   └── skills/
   264→│       └── [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
   265→├── tasks/                           (task files go here)
   266→├── CLAUDE.md                        (main workflow guide)
   267→└── README.md                        (project documentation)
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   271→
   272→**Verify setup:**
   273→```bash
   274→# Check git hook exists and is executable
   275→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   276→
   277→# Check automation files exist
   278→ls -la .claude/
   279→
   280→# Make hook executable (if needed)
   281→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   282→```
   283→
   284→### For New Developers/Contributors
   285→
   286→1. **Clone repository**
   287→2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
   288→3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   289→4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
   290→5. **Test git hook:**
   291→   ```bash
   292→   # Make a small test change
   293→   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx  # or index.js, etc.
   294→
   295→   # Try to commit without staging task file
   296→   git add src/main.tsx
   297→   git commit -m "test"
   298→   # Should be BLOCKED
   299→
   300→   # Stage task file and retry
   301→   git add tasks/some-task.md
   302→   git commit -m "test"
   303→   # Should succeed
   304→
   305→   # Revert test
   306→   git reset HEAD~1
   307→   git checkout src/main.tsx
   308→   ```
   309→
   310→---
   311→
   312→## How It Works
   313→
   314→### Git Hook Workflow
   315→
   316→```
   317→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   318→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   319→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   320→                     │
   321→                     ▼
   322→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   323→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   324→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   325→                     │
   326→                     ▼
   327→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   328→│  Check: Are any code files staged?                          │
   329→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/, lib/)    │
   330→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
   331→             │ YES                    │ NO
   332→             ▼                        ▼
   333→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   334→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   335→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   336→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   337→       │ YES       │ NO
   338→       ▼           ▼
   339→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   340→│ Allow      │  │ BLOCK commit                                │
   341→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   342→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   343→```
   344→
   345→### Task File Lifecycle
   346→
   347→```
   348→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   349→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   350→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   351→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   352→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   353→                     │
   354→                     ▼
   355→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   356→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   357→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   358→│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
   359→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   360→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   361→                     │
   362→                     ▼
   363→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   364→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   365→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   366→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   367→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   368→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   369→                     │
   370→                     ▼
   371→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   372→│  4. Completion                                               │
   373→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   374→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   375→│     - Add testing status                                     │
   376→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   377→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   378→                     │
   379→                     ▼
   380→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   381→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   382→│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
   383→│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
   384→│     - Push to remote: git push origin [branch]              │
   385→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   386→```
   387→
   388→---
   389→
   390→## Troubleshooting
   391→
   392→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   393→
   394→**Symptoms:**
   395→- Can commit code without task file
   396→- No error message appears
   397→
   398→**Causes & Fixes:**
   399→
   400→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   401→```bash
   402→# Fix: Make it executable
   403→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   404→```
   405→
   406→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   407→```bash
   408→# Check if file exists
   409→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   410→
   411→# If missing, recreate from template
   412→```
   413→
   414→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   415→```bash
   416→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   417→git commit  # Correct
   418→git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
   419→```
   420→
   421→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   422→```bash
   423→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
   424→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   425→# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
   426→```
   427→
   428→---
   429→
   430→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   431→
   432→**Symptoms:**
   433→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   434→- False positive error
   435→
   436→**Causes & Fixes:**
   437→
   438→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   439→```bash
   440→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   441→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
   442→git add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
   443→git add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
   444→```
   445→
   446→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   447→```bash
   448→# Check what's staged
   449→git diff --cached --name-only
   450→
   451→# Ensure task file is in the list
   452→# If not, stage it:
   453→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   454→```
   455→
   456→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   457→```bash
   458→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   459→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
   460→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
   461→```
   462→
   463→---
   464→
   465→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   466→
   467→**Symptoms:**
   468→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   469→- File not found error
   470→
   471→**Fixes:**
   472→```bash
   473→# Check if template exists
   474→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   475→
   476→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   477→ls -la .claude/
   478→
   479→# Ensure you're in repository root
   480→pwd
   481→```
   482→
   483→---
   484→
   485→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   486→
   487→**Symptoms:**
   488→- Code commits go through without task file
   489→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   490→
   491→**Causes & Fixes:**
   492→
   493→**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
   494→```bash
   495→# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
   496→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   497→
   498→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   499→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   500→# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
   501→```
   502→
   503→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   504→```bash
   505→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   506→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   507→# This is intentional behavior
   508→```
   509→
   510→---
   511→
   512→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   513→
   514→**Symptoms:**
   515→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   516→- Silent failure
   517→
   518→**Fixes:**
   519→```bash
   520→# Test hook manually
   521→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   522→
   523→# Check for syntax errors
   524→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   525→
   526→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   527→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   528→```
   529→
   530→---
   531→
   532→## Customization
   533→
   534→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   535→
   536→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   537→
   538→```bash
   539→# Current pattern (example)
   540→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   541→
   542→# Add more directories
   543→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   544→```
   545→
   546→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   547→
   548→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   549→
   550→```bash
   551→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   552→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
   553→```
   554→
   555→### Customizing Task Template
   556→
   557→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   558→
   559→**Example additions:**
   560→```markdown
   561→## Security Checklist
   562→- [ ] Input validation added
   563→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   564→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   565→- [ ] CSRF protection verified
   566→
   567→## Performance Checklist
   568→- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
   569→- [ ] No memory leaks
   570→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   571→- [ ] Lazy loading where appropriate
   572→
   573→## Accessibility Checklist
   574→- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
   575→- [ ] Screen reader compatible
   576→- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
   577→- [ ] Focus indicators visible
   578→
   579→## Browser/Device Testing
   580→- [ ] Chrome
   581→- [ ] Firefox
   582→- [ ] Safari
   583→- [ ] Edge
   584→- [ ] Mobile (375px)
   585→- [ ] Tablet (768px)
   586→- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
   587→```
   588→
   589→### Adding New Custom Commands
   590→
   591→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   592→
   593→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   594→```markdown
   595→---
   596→description: Review task before marking complete
   597→---
   598→
   599→# Review Task Checklist
   600→
   601→Before marking task complete:
   602→
   603→- [ ] Review section filled out
   604→- [ ] All files documented
   605→- [ ] Testing complete
   606→- [ ] Documentation updated
   607→- [ ] Bundle size checked
   608→```
   609→
   610→### Adding More Skills
   611→
   612→1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
   613→2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
   614→3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
   615→4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`
   616→
   617→---
   618→
   619→## Best Practices
   620→
   621→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   622→
   623→```bash
   624→cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
   625→# Or use custom command
   626→/start-task
   627→```
   628→
   629→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   630→
   631→```bash
   632→# CORRECT ORDER
   633→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   634→# Fill out the task file
   635→# THEN start coding
   636→
   637→# WRONG ORDER
   638→# Start coding first
   639→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   640→```
   641→
   642→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   643→
   644→```markdown
   645→## Todo Checklist
   646→
   647→### Phase 1
   648→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
   649→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   650→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
   651→```
   652→
   653→### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately
   654→
   655→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   656→- Summary of changes
   657→- Files created/modified
   658→- Testing status
   659→- Issues encountered
   660→
   661→Don't wait - you'll forget important details!
   662→
   663→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   664→
   665→```bash
   666→# CORRECT - Stage both together
   667→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   668→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   669→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   670→
   671→# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
   672→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   673→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   674→```
   675→
   676→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   677→
   678→```bash
   679→# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   680→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   681→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   682→tasks/refactor-state-stores.md
   683→
   684→# BAD - Vague, generic
   685→tasks/todo.md
   686→tasks/task1.md
   687→tasks/stuff.md
   688→```
   689→
   690→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   691→
   692→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   693→
   694→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   695→
   696→```bash
   697→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   698→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   699→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   700→git commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix
   701→
   702→# NOT for:
   703→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   704→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
   705→```
   706→
   707→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   708→
   709→If architecture changes, update docs:
   710→```bash
   711→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   712→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   713→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
   714→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
   715→```
   716→
   717→### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes
   718→
   719→For ANY visual/UI change:
   720→1. Screenshot current state
   721→2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
   722→3. Screenshot after
   723→4. Compare to baseline
   724→5. Get user approval before committing
   725→
   726→### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run
   727→
   728→```bash
   729→# CORRECT
   730→DRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
   731→# Verify output looks good
   732→# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution
   733→
   734→# WRONG
   735→# Just run live and hope it works
   736→```
   737→
   738→### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages
   739→
   740→```bash
   741→# GOOD
   742→git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link
   743→
   744→- Added new link to social menu
   745→- Updated animation timing
   746→- Tested on all browsers
   747→
   748→Generated with Claude Code
   749→Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"
   750→
   751→# BAD
   752→git commit -m "update stuff"
   753→```
   754→
   755→---
   756→
   757→## Project-Specific Configurations
   758→
   759→### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)
   760→
   761→- No build tools required
   762→- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
   763→- Focus on browser compatibility testing
   764→- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading
   765→
   766→### React/Vue/Angular Projects
   767→
   768→- Run `npm run test` before commits
   769→- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
   770→- Run `npm run lint` for code style
   771→- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
   772→- Test in light mode AND dark mode
   773→
   774→### Full-Stack Projects
   775→
   776→- Test both frontend and backend changes
   777→- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
   778→- Check database migrations
   779→- Test authentication flows
   780→
   781→### Automation Projects
   782→
   783→- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
   784→- Verify API keys are properly stored
   785→- Test error handling
   786→- Document required environment variables
   787→
   788→---
   789→
   790→## Integration with Claude Code
   791→
   792→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   793→
   794→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   795→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   796→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   797→4. **Fill review section** when done
   798→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   799→
   800→### Behavioral Guidelines
   801→
   802→**Before starting ANY task:**
   803→- Read `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
   804→- Create task file from template
   805→- Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   806→
   807→**During implementation:**
   808→- Update todos as you complete them
   809→- Document decisions in task file
   810→- Follow simplicity principle
   811→
   812→**When complete:**
   813→- Fill out review section
   814→- Update docs/ if architecture changed
   815→- Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   816→
   817→**On every commit:**
   818→- Stage code files AND task file together
   819→- Git hook verifies compliance
   820→- Commit proceeds
   821→
   822→### Using Skills
   823→
   824→Available skills can be invoked explicitly:
   825→```
   826→Use [skill-name] to [action]
   827→```
   828→
   829→---
   830→
   831→## Appendix
   832→
   833→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   834→
   835→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   836→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   837→
   838→### ANSI Color Codes Used
   839→
   840→```bash
   841→RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   842→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   843→GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   844→BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   845→NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   846→```
   847→
   848→---
   849→
   850→## See Also
   851→
   852→- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
   853→- `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
   854→- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
   855→- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
   856→- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands
   857→
   858→---
   859→
   860→**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
   861→**Last Updated:** December 2025
   862→
   863→**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development across ANY project.
   864→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

### Supported Project Types

This guide supports multiple project configurations:

| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
|--------------|------------|-------------|
| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |

---

## Quick Start

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with main branch:**
   ```bash
   # For main branch
   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit

   # For master branch
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**
   ```bash
   cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

3. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
   Use `ExitPlanMode` tool or ask user before coding

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Or: npx serve .
# Visit: http://localhost:8000
```

### For Node.js/React Projects

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start development server
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm run test

# Type check (TypeScript)
npm run type-check

# Build
npm run build
```

### For Automation Scripts

```bash
# Test with dry run first
DRY_RUN=true python automation/[script].py

# Verify output before running live
```

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All workflow rules

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- During Implementation
- When Complete
- Git Commit Checklist
- Quick Reference for all rules

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`
- `tasks/optimize-image-loading.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- `tasks/todo.md`
- `tasks/task1.md`
- `tasks/new.md`
- `tasks/stuff.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Intelligent task router

**What it covers:**
1. Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
2. Creates task files from templates
3. Checks existing todos
4. Initiates planning mode
5. Gets user approval before implementation

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

#### `/run` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/run.md`
**Purpose:** Execute prompts with auto-archiving

---

## Setup Instructions

### File Structure (Generic)

```
[project-root]/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md                   (global instructions)
│   ├── README.md                   (folder overview)
│   ├── settings.json               (Claude settings)
│   ├── settings.local.json         (local overrides)
│   ├── docs/
│   │   ├── automation-guide.md     (this file)
│   │   └── workflow-checklist.md   (step-by-step checklist)
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   └── start-task.md           (custom commands)
│   └── skills/
│       └── [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
├── tasks/                           (task files go here)
├── CLAUDE.md                        (main workflow guide)
└── README.md                        (project documentation)
```

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Make hook executable (if needed)
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

### For New Developers/Contributors

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small test change
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx  # or index.js, etc.

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any code files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/, lib/)    │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Allow      │  │ BLOCK commit                                │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status                                     │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
│     - Push to remote: git push origin [branch]              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # Correct
git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
git add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
```bash
# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)
# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Current pattern (example)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented
- [ ] CSRF protection verified

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
- [ ] Lazy loading where appropriate

## Accessibility Checklist
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader compatible
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Focus indicators visible

## Browser/Device Testing
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari
- [ ] Edge
- [ ] Mobile (375px)
- [ ] Tablet (768px)
- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
```markdown
---
description: Review task before marking complete
---

# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adding More Skills

1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

```bash
cat .claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md
# Or use custom command
/start-task
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
```

### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-state-stores.md

# BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
```

### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes

For ANY visual/UI change:
1. Screenshot current state
2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
3. Screenshot after
4. Compare to baseline
5. Get user approval before committing

### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run

```bash
# CORRECT
DRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
# Verify output looks good
# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution

# WRONG
# Just run live and hope it works
```

### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages

```bash
# GOOD
git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link

- Added new link to social menu
- Updated animation timing
- Tested on all browsers

Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"

# BAD
git commit -m "update stuff"
```

---

## Project-Specific Configurations

### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)

- No build tools required
- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
- Focus on browser compatibility testing
- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading

### React/Vue/Angular Projects

- Run `npm run test` before commits
- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
- Run `npm run lint` for code style
- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
- Test in light mode AND dark mode

### Full-Stack Projects

- Test both frontend and backend changes
- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
- Check database migrations
- Test authentication flows

### Automation Projects

- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
- Verify API keys are properly stored
- Test error handling
- Document required environment variables

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Guidelines

**Before starting ANY task:**
- Read `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md`
- Create task file from template
- Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- Update todos as you complete them
- Document decisions in task file
- Follow simplicity principle

**When complete:**
- Fill out review section
- Update docs/ if architecture changed
- Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- Stage code files AND task file together
- Git hook verifies compliance
- Commit proceeds

### Using Skills

Available skills can be invoked explicitly:
```
Use [skill-name] to [action]
```

---

## Appendix

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands

**Last Updated:** December 2025

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development across ANY project.

input.content:
# Workflow Automation Guide

**Version:** 4.0 (Consolidated)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Complete workflow compliance guide - checklists + automation tools

---

## Quick Navigation

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [Quick Checklists](#quick-checklists) | Pre-flight, implementation, completion |
| [Automation Tools](#automation-tools) | Git hooks, templates, commands |
| [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions) | Initial configuration |
| [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) | Common issues and fixes |
| [Customization](#customization) | Extend and modify tools |
| [Best Practices](#best-practices) | Tips for success |

---

# PART 1: Quick Checklists

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
  ```bash
  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
  # OR: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
  ```
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**

---

## Project-Type Checklists

### For UI/Visual Changes

- [ ] Screenshot current state before changes
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test in browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Test responsiveness: 375px, 768px, 1024px+
- [ ] Compare to baseline (visual diff)
- [ ] Check animations work (60fps target)
- [ ] Get user approval before committing

### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects

- [ ] Run tests: `npm run test`
- [ ] Type check: `npm run type-check`
- [ ] Lint check: `npm run lint`
- [ ] Build check: `npm run build`
- [ ] Verify bundle size acceptable

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

- [ ] Manual verification in browser
- [ ] Check console for errors
- [ ] Resize window (mobile/desktop)
- [ ] Run Lighthouse audit in DevTools

### For Automation/Scripts

- [ ] Test with `DRY_RUN=true` first
- [ ] Verify output matches expectations
- [ ] Check validation rules work
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md`
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed
- [ ] **Verify in light mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation** (accessibility)
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes**

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md`
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

---

## Quick Reference Tables

### Core Workflow Rules

| # | Rule | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | **Think → Plan → Write** | Create task file FIRST before coding |
| 2 | **Plan has todo list** | Checklist with items to check off |
| 3 | **Check before working** | Get user approval via ExitPlanMode |
| 4 | **Mark tasks complete** | Update todos as you go |
| 5 | **High-level explanations** | Don't overexplain every detail |
| 6 | **Simple changes** | Minimal code impact |
| 7 | **Add review section** | Document what changed when done |
| 8 | **No lazy fixes** | Find root cause, no temporary patches |
| 9 | **Simplicity is key** | Impact minimal code, avoid bugs |
| 10 | **Update documentation** | Keep docs detailed, clear, clean |
| 11 | **Sync with remote** | Start of EVERY session |
| 12 | **Perfect foundation** | Build for 100% even if 1% complete |
| 13 | **Complete logical units** | Never stop mid-implementation |
| 14 | **Validate current date** | Check `<env>` section |
| 15 | **Commit & Push at end** | Git add, commit with details, push |

### Core Philosophy Principles

| # | Principle | Description |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | **Correctness & Integrity** | No data loss. Security P0. |
| 2 | **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
| 3 | **Atomic Execution** | Small, reviewable steps. Explain WHY. |
| 4 | **Maintainability** | Explicit > Clever. No magic. |
| 5 | **Optimal Location** | Files must be in the most logical place. |
| 6 | **Scalable Architecture** | Build primitives, not one-offs. |
| 7 | **Root Cause Only** | Trace bugs to source. No band-aids. |
| 8 | **Infinite Initiative** | Fix broken windows immediately. |

---

# PART 2: Automation Tools

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

```

```

## Tool 2: Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files

│   ├── commands/
│   │   └── start-task.md           (custom commands)
│   └── skills/

├── tasks/                          (task files go here)
├── CLAUDE.md                       (main workflow guide)
└── README.md                       (project documentation)
```

## Initial Setup

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Make hook executable (if needed)
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

## For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small test change
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

# PART 4: Troubleshooting

## Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:** Can commit code without task file

**Fixes:**

```bash
# Cause 1: Hook not executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Cause 2: Hook file doesn't exist
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Cause 3: Using --no-verify flag (don't do this)
git commit  # Correct
git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
```

## Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:** Hook blocks even with task file staged

**Fixes:**

```bash
# Cause 1: Task file in wrong location
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
git add my-feature.md         # Wrong

# Cause 2: Task file not staged
git diff --cached --name-only  # Check what's staged

# Cause 3: Wrong file extension
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct (.md)
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong
```

## Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd
```

---

# PART 5: Customization

## Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Add more directories to monitor
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

## Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add sections like:
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps

## Accessibility Checklist
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader compatible
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
```

## Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

```markdown
---
description: Your command description
---

# Command Content Here
```

---

# PART 6: Best Practices

## 1. Always Start with the Checklist

```bash
# View this file or use command
/start-task
```

## 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

## 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked
```

## 4. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

## 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# WRONG - Hook will block
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
```

## 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# GOOD
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md

# BAD
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
```

## 7. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
# - Hotfix (site down)
# - Revert broken commit
# - Critical security fix

# NOT for:
# - "I'm lazy"
# - "I'll update task file later"
```

# Workflow Automation Guide

**Version:** 4.0 (Consolidated)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Complete workflow compliance guide - checklists + automation tools

---

## Quick Navigation

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [Quick Checklists](#quick-checklists) | Pre-flight, implementation, completion |
| [Automation Tools](#automation-tools) | Git hooks, templates, commands |
| [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions) | Initial configuration |
| [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) | Common issues and fixes |
| [Customization](#customization) | Extend and modify tools |
| [Best Practices](#best-practices) | Tips for success |

---

# PART 1: Quick Checklists

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
  ```bash
  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
  # OR: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
  ```
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**

---

## Project-Type Checklists

### For UI/Visual Changes

- [ ] Screenshot current state before changes
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test in browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Test responsiveness: 375px, 768px, 1024px+
- [ ] Compare to baseline (visual diff)
- [ ] Check animations work (60fps target)
- [ ] Get user approval before committing

### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects

- [ ] Run tests: `npm run test`
- [ ] Type check: `npm run type-check`
- [ ] Lint check: `npm run lint`
- [ ] Build check: `npm run build`
- [ ] Verify bundle size acceptable

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

- [ ] Manual verification in browser
- [ ] Check console for errors
- [ ] Resize window (mobile/desktop)
- [ ] Run Lighthouse audit in DevTools

### For Automation/Scripts

- [ ] Test with `DRY_RUN=true` first
- [ ] Verify output matches expectations
- [ ] Check validation rules work
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md`
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed
- [ ] **Verify in light mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation** (accessibility)
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes**

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md`
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

---

## Quick Reference Tables

### Core Workflow Rules

| # | Rule | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | **Think → Plan → Write** | Create task file FIRST before coding |
| 2 | **Plan has todo list** | Checklist with items to check off |
| 3 | **Check before working** | Get user approval via ExitPlanMode |
| 4 | **Mark tasks complete** | Update todos as you go |
| 5 | **High-level explanations** | Don't overexplain every detail |
| 6 | **Simple changes** | Minimal code impact |
| 7 | **Add review section** | Document what changed when done |
| 8 | **No lazy fixes** | Find root cause, no temporary patches |
| 9 | **Simplicity is key** | Impact minimal code, avoid bugs |
| 10 | **Update documentation** | Keep docs detailed, clear, clean |
| 11 | **Sync with remote** | Start of EVERY session |
| 12 | **Perfect foundation** | Build for 100% even if 1% complete |
| 13 | **Complete logical units** | Never stop mid-implementation |
| 14 | **Validate current date** | Check `<env>` section |
| 15 | **Commit & Push at end** | Git add, commit with details, push |

### Core Philosophy Principles

| # | Principle | Description |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | **Correctness & Integrity** | No data loss. Security P0. |
| 2 | **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
| 3 | **Atomic Execution** | Small, reviewable steps. Explain WHY. |
| 4 | **Maintainability** | Explicit > Clever. No magic. |
| 5 | **Optimal Location** | Files must be in the most logical place. |
| 6 | **Scalable Architecture** | Build primitives, not one-offs. |
| 7 | **Root Cause Only** | Trace bugs to source. No band-aids. |
| 8 | **Infinite Initiative** | Fix broken windows immediately. |

---

# Claude Code Skills Library

Custom skills for Claude Code - reusable across any project.

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

---

## Generic Skills (Use for Any Project)

### Development Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **generic-code-reviewer** | Multi-stack code review | Before commits, PR reviews |
| **generic-feature-developer** | Architecture patterns | Adding features, planning changes |
| **generic-design-system** | Design tokens & patterns | UI implementation, styling |
| **generic-ux-designer** | UX best practices | Design reviews, user flows |

### Utility Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **codebase-documenter** | Generate documentation | README, API docs, comments |
| **tech-debt-analyzer** | Code health analysis | Audits, refactoring planning |
| **test-specialist** | Testing guidance | Writing tests, debugging |
| **frontend-enhancer** | Frontend patterns | UI improvements |
| **document-skills** | Office docs (docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf) | Document creation |
| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |

---

## Example Skills (Customization Templates)

These skills demonstrate how to customize for specific project types. Copy and modify for your own projects.

### example-static-site-* (Static HTML/CSS/JS Sites)
- **example-static-site-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, performance focus
- **example-static-site-design-system** - Minimalist design patterns
- **example-static-site-feature-developer** - Landing page patterns
- **example-static-site-ux-designer** - Lightweight UX constraints

### example-react-app-* (React/TypeScript Applications)
- **example-react-app-code-reviewer** - React/TypeScript, privacy-first
- **example-react-app-design-system** - Dark mode, accessibility focus
- **example-react-app-feature-developer** - Local-first architecture
- **example-react-app-ux-designer** - Productivity app UX

### example-fullstack-* (Full-Stack Next.js/NestJS)
- **example-fullstack-code-reviewer** - Next.js + NestJS, Prisma
- **example-fullstack-design-system** - Modern theme patterns
- **example-fullstack-feature-developer** - Full-stack patterns
- **example-fullstack-ux-designer** - SaaS app UX

---

## How to Use Skills

### Automatic Activation
Skills activate when relevant context appears in conversation.

### Explicit Invocation
Request skills directly:
```
Use generic-code-reviewer to review this code
Use generic-design-system for the color choices
Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
```

### Skill Tool
```
Skill(generic-code-reviewer)
Skill(tech-debt-analyzer)
```

---

## Skill Structure

Each skill follows this structure:

```
skills/
└── [skill-name]/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
```

### SKILL.md Format

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
---

# Skill Title

Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
```

---

## Creating New Skills

### 1. Create Folder
```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]
```

### 2. Create SKILL.md
- YAML frontmatter with name and description
- Comprehensive content for Claude to follow
- Examples and patterns

### 3. Add References (Optional)
Place in `references/` folder for additional context.

### 4. Test
Verify skill activates when expected context appears.

---

## Skill Categories

### By Tech Stack

| Stack | Recommended Skills |
|-------|-------------------|
| HTML/CSS/JS | generic-*, example-static-site-* |
| React/TypeScript | generic-*, example-react-app-* |
| Next.js/NestJS | generic-*, example-fullstack-* |
| Python | generic-code-reviewer, tech-debt-analyzer |
| Any | codebase-documenter, test-specialist |

### By Task Type

| Task | Recommended Skills |
|------|-------------------|
| Code review | generic-code-reviewer, [project]-code-reviewer |
| New feature | generic-feature-developer |
| UI/Styling | generic-design-system |
| UX design | generic-ux-designer |
| Documentation | codebase-documenter |
| Code health | tech-debt-analyzer |
| Testing | test-specialist |
| Documents | document-skills |

---

## Skills vs Other Claude Code Features

| Feature | Use For |
|---------|---------|
| **Skills** | Domain knowledge, guidelines, patterns |
| **Slash Commands** | Quick actions, workflows |
| **Hooks** | Automated responses to events |
| **Subagents** | Complex multi-step tasks |
| **MCP Servers** | External tool integration |

---

## Best Practices

### When to Create a Skill
- Repeated guidance needed
- Project-specific standards
- Complex domain knowledge
- Reusable patterns

### When NOT to Create a Skill
- One-time instructions
- Simple preferences (use CLAUDE.md)
- External tool integration (use MCP)

### Skill Content Tips
- Be comprehensive but focused
- Include concrete examples
- Reference other skills when relevant
- Keep updated as project evolves

---

## See Also

- `.claude/docs/automation-guide.md` - Workflow automation
- `.claude/docs/workflow-checklist.md` - Development checklist
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom slash commands
- `.claude/templates/` - Task templates
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules

---

## Questions?

- Skills not activating? Check skill name and description match context
- Need new skill? Follow existing patterns in this folder
- Skill conflicts? Review descriptions for proper use cases
